What will happen with this is that the color of my document will turn green. So if I write here document.body.style.background color. Now here the CSS properties are given by their counterparts. The background color will be written like this. Background-color will not be written. And if I write green here, then it will put an inline CSS. So I will get to see this inline CSS here. So you can see that the background color is given in green with the help of inline CSS. So you can inject the inline CSS. So this is our document object model. Now there is a DOM, browser object model. Now knowing about DOM and DOM will not give you a lot of power. But it is asked in the interview. What is a document object model? Mapping is the inner object of the entire document. The object in which you get all the content of the page. And what is the DOM? Browser object model. Now if you want to work with anyone other than the document, then the browser provides you some additional objects. It is called DOM. Like location.href is provided to you. So that you can redirect to another URL. Which is your window object. If I write window here, then there is a window object. In which you have a document. And if I write here window.document, window.document, then you get the document. If I write window.document.body, then you get the body. Now the window is a global object. So you will right click and click on inspect. So you see, I inspected this element by right clicking. So this element was selected. I can use the $0 to refer this object. So if I write $0 here, I write $0. So you see that this element is coming, which I selected. So whatever is selected becomes $0. Now if I write style.backgroundcolor and I write equals to green. So I can make its background color green. As you can see. So what is this $0? Whichever element you selected was $0. Whichever you clicked here is $0. Now if I want to make it $0, I will right click and inspect. And whichever element I click here, it will become $0. You see, this section with the thumbnail is $0. Here this container is $0. So if I run $0 style.backgroundcolor green now, then this one will be green. So $0 is inside the browser. So the browser gives you this kind of utility so that you can select a particular element and can add styles in it. So this is our document object model. Now I want to show you this image which will represent DOM better and will explain to you what exactly DOM is. So in the document, we have HTML, which has head and body. The body has that thing that we see. And here you can see that every element has a leaf node. It is either text or an empty element. So what you have to do here is if you want to select any element, then you have to go through DOM. It is possible that you want to search from a particular node. Suppose I want to get any div inside the body. So there are functions for that. If you want a given ID or a given class element inside the body, then you can select it. If you want to select multiple elements, then you can do that. In all these things, it provides you with the facility to select and use any complex query to select different elements. You can use CSS selectors within JavaScript. If you ever want to use multiple nodes with a particular CSS selector, then it's fine. So I hope you understood about DOM. If you want to learn more about DOM, then this is a page of MDN in which it is said that DOM represents a logical tree. Every branch ends in a node. And this is a representation like an object. Now, until we do this, you won't understand it. So what we will do is I will show you by searching in the coming videos and I will show you how elements are targeted. I just showed you a very small example here. But in the coming videos, we will do more complex things. Then you will understand how to target different elements with the help of JavaScript and maybe change their CSS or remove them from DOM or add them to DOM. All these things are done. So I hope you liked this video and you are getting to learn things. That's all for now, guys. If you haven't accessed the playlist, then do it. Thank you so much guys for watching this video. And I will see you next time. Bye. Outro Music.
